text: Hello and welcome to Real Easy English.
zh: 大家好，欢迎收听《Real Easy English》。

text: In this podcast, we have real conversations in Easy English to help you learn.
zh: 在这个播客节目中，我们用简单英语进行真实对话，帮助大家学习英语。

text: I'm Neil.
zh: 我是尼尔。

text: And I'm Georgie.
zh: 我是乔治。

text: Remember, you can watch a video version of this podcast on our website,
zh: 记得哦，你可以在我们的网站上观看这个播客的视频版本，

text: bbclearningenglish .com How are you today, Georgie?
zh: 网址是bbclearningenglish.com。乔治，你今天好吗？

text: I'm very well, thank you, today.
zh: 我今天很好，谢谢。

text: Neil, how are you?
zh: 尼尔，你呢？

text: I'm OK.
zh: 我还好。

text: I've got one or two little worries.
zh: 就是有一两件小烦心事。

text: Have you?
zh: 是吗？

text: Well, that's interesting because we are talking about worries today in this podcast.
zh: 这可有意思了，因为我们今天这期播客正好要聊“烦恼”这个话题。

text: Worries are problems that make you feel upset or scared.
zh: 烦恼指的是让你感到难过或害怕的问题。

text: So, shall we start the conversation?
zh: 那我们开始对话吧？

text: Let's do it.
zh: 来吧。

text: So, Neil, you said you've got a couple of little worries.
zh: 尼尔，你说你有一两件小烦心事，

text: Do you want to share them?
zh: 要不要跟大家分享一下？

text: Yeah.
zh: 好啊。

text: I think when you have kids, you're kind of slightly worried all the time.
zh: 我觉得有了孩子之后，你就会一直有点担心。

text: And Yesterday my son got hurt playing cricket and so this morning I just wanted to make sure he was feeling okay before he went to school.
zh: 昨天我儿子打板球的时候受伤了，所以今天早上他上学前，我特意想确认他感觉还好。

text: And how was he this morning?
zh: 那他今天早上怎么样？

text: He seemed okay.
zh: 看起来还好。

text: Yeah?
zh: 是吗？

text: He seemed a bit bruised.
zh: 看起来有点淤青。

text: Oh no.
zh: 哦，不。

text: An injury to the knee or something?
zh: 是膝盖受伤了还是怎么？

text: Shoulder.
zh: 是肩膀。

text: Ouch.
zh: 哎哟。

text: And arm.
zh: 还有胳膊。

text: Gosh.
zh: 天哪。

text: Yeah.
zh: 是啊。

text: And are you someone that worries a lot?
zh: 那你是那种容易担心的人吗？

text: No, not really.
zh: 不，不太是。

text: Sometimes, but I think there's lots of stuff that you can worry about and if you worry about all of it, it can really affect you badly.
zh: 有时候会吧，但我觉得值得担心的事情太多了，如果每件事都担心，会对自己造成很大的负面影响。

text: So I try, I know it's difficult sometimes, not to worry too much about things that aren't so important.
zh: 所以我尽量——虽然有时候挺难的——不去为那些不太重要的事情过度担心。

text: My thing is that worrying about a problem or a thing doesn't actually help.
zh: 我的想法是，为一个问题或一件事担心，其实并没有什么帮助。

text: So I'm, in general, I'm a person that doesn't worry too much.
zh: 所以总体来说，我不是那种爱操心的人。

text: I kind of just think whatever happens happens.
zh: 我就是那种“该发生的就会发生”的心态。

text: So I'm generally quite chilled and relaxed and Yeah,
zh: 所以我一般都挺淡定、挺放松的，嗯，

text: my friend always Says that to me because she's someone that worries a lot about everything travel You know all of those things and she always says how are you always so relaxed about things that go wrong, but?
zh: 我朋友总是这么跟我说，因为她是那种什么事都爱担心的人，比如旅行之类的，她总说，你怎么对那些出岔子的事总能这么淡定？

text: You can't change you can't change them.
zh: 你没法改变的事情，就是没法改变啊。

text: So that's true.
zh: 这倒是真的。

text: That's a very healthy attitude probably So I don't tend to worry a lot, but you worry a little bit because you have kids.
zh: 这可能是一种很健康的心态。所以我一般不太爱担心，不过你会因为有孩子而担心一点。

text: What kinds of things do you do to make yourself feel more relaxed?
zh: 那你会做些什么来让自己放松一点呢？

text: Well, I think like you said, one useful thing to do could be to try to be rational.
zh: 嗯，我觉得就像你说的，一个有用的办法就是尽量理性一点，

text: So to think that what you're worried about probably isn't that important or likely to happen yeah
zh: 想想你担心的事情，很可能没那么重要，也不太可能真的发生，对。

text: so you're quite chilled as you said that must be nice
zh: 所以你确实挺淡定的，这对你朋友来说一定很省心。

text: for your friends yes and if you have like you said a friend who worries about everything how do you help them finding maybe a distraction like something that makes you feel good like
zh: 是啊，如果像你说的，有个朋友什么事都担心，你会怎么帮她呢？也许可以找点让自己分心的事，比如

text: a TV program that you love or seeing friends that love you that can make you feel more relaxed mindfulness techniques as well well, maybe jigsaws.
zh: 看一部你喜欢的电视节目，或者见见让你感到开心的朋友，这些都能让人更放松，正念练习也可以，或者拼拼图。

text: I've talked about puzzles in the past.
zh: 我之前也提到过拼图这类的事情。

text: Do you have any other tips?
zh: 你还有别的建议吗？

text: No, these are all very good ideas.
zh: 没了，这些都是很好的主意。

text: Thank you.
zh: 谢谢。

text: Yeah, I think it can be useful to have something that distracts you, like maybe drawing, listening to music, playing an instrument can help.
zh: 是啊，我觉得找点能转移注意力的事情会挺有用，比如画画、听音乐、演奏乐器，都能有帮助。

text: Something that just kind of takes that time away.
zh: 就是那种能让你暂时抽离出来的事情。

text: It takes your mind off the stress.
zh: 能让你的注意力从压力上转移开。

text: OK, let's recap the vocabulary we heard during the conversation, starting with worry.
zh: 好，我们来回顾一下这次对话中出现的词汇，先从“worry”开始。

text: A worry is a problem that makes you feel upset or scared.
zh: “worry”是指让你感到难过或害怕的问题。

text: And worry is also a verb.
zh: “worry”也可以作动词用。

text: So, for example, I worry about my kids.
zh: 比如说，“I worry about my kids”（我担心我的孩子）。

text: Yes, and we also use the adjective worried to describe that feeling of being scared or upset about something.
zh: 是的，我们还会用形容词“worried”来形容那种因为某事而感到害怕或难过的心情。

text: For example, I'm worried about climate change.
zh: 比如，“I'm worried about climate change”（我很担心气候变化）。

text: And we heard the expression take your mind off something, which means to do something to stop you worrying.
zh: 我们还听到了“take your mind off something”这个表达，意思是做点什么事来让自己不再担心。

text: For example, I play my guitar to take my mind off my worries.
zh: 比如，“I play my guitar to take my mind off my worries”（我弹吉他来让自己不去想那些烦心事）。

text: We also heard chilled, which is another word for relaxed or calm, and it's quite an informal word.
zh: 我们还听到了“chilled”这个词，是“relaxed”或“calm”的另一种说法，是个比较口语化的词。

text: That's it for this episode of Real Easy English.
zh: 好，本期《Real Easy English》就到这里。

text: Try the worksheet on our website to test what you've learned.
zh: 去我们的网站试试练习题，测试一下你学到的内容吧。

text: Go to bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 网址是bbclearningenglish.com。

text: See you then.
zh: 我们下次再见。

text: Goodbye.
zh: 再见。
